exposure.description: How the service is meant to be reached by an operator or consumer. `url` remains the primary canonical URL; `exposure` adds intent so adopters can validate that operator-visible URLs are not local implementation details. Protocol-level fields stay vendor-neutral; adopter-specific resolver/proxy names belong in downstream profiles.

visibility.description: `operator` means the `url` is meant for a real client/operator; `local` means loopback or host-local access only; `hidden` means consumers may keep the service out of launch/navigation surfaces.

canonical.description: Whether `url` is the canonical operator-facing address for this service.

backend_url.description: Optional implementation/backend address used by a proxy or runbook. This may be loopback; it is not the operator-facing URL.

interface.description: Kind of interface the service exposes. Recommended values: web, api, mqtt, tcp, ssh, none, other. Optional; defaults to 'web' for backward compatibility. SHOULD be set explicitly when the service does not serve HTML at the listed url — including HTTP APIs without HTML (MCP, REST, GraphQL, JSON-RPC), which should declare 'api'. The earlier wording of this rule (0.2.0) covered only non-http(s) URLs and missed HTTPS APIs without HTML; broadened in 0.3.1 (see SPEC.md and DOWNSTREAM_FEEDBACK.md DF-004).

secrets_source.description: Reference to the external source of secret values for this service. Secret values themselves are forbidden. The protocol names a generic source shape; adopters may constrain `kind` to their approved store (for example Doppler, SOPS, 1Password, Vault).

deployment.description: Optional declaration of the operator's intent for what should be running. Catalog declares intent only; evidence about runtime state is read by consumers from the live system, never edited into the catalog. See SPEC.md *Service / deployment* (and the adjacent *Deployment lifecycle vocabulary*, *Rule: "operationally deployed"*, *Rule: intent vs evidence* sub-sections) and docs/DEPLOYMENT_EVIDENCE_PROPOSAL.md for the contract.

pattern.description: Optional declaration of the deployment pattern the service follows, such as `upstream-docker`, `custom-image`, or an adopter-defined profile. This records intent; runtime evidence still comes from consumers.

deviations.description: Documented deviations from the declared deployment pattern, for example overriding an upstream entrypoint. Empty array means no known intentional deviations.

expected.description: What the operator expects to be deployed. Sub-blocks are independent: a service may declare `image` only, `health` only, both, or neither.

image.description: Image tag (or other artefact reference) the operator expects to be running. Omit for services without a controllable image (Cloudflare tunnels, third-party SaaS).

health.description: How a consumer reaches a version-bearing endpoint. Omit for services without one (e.g. mosquitto, vaultwarden).

url.description: Endpoint URL the consumer probes for the running version.

version_json_path.description: JSONPath into the response body that yields the running version string (e.g. $.version).

version.description: Expected value at the JSONPath. Drift is reported when the live response yields a different value.
